rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative intramolecular cyclization of alkenyl alcohols with external nucleophiles (diarylmethanimines or carboxylic acids) to access amino- and hydroxy-functionalized tetrahydrofurans/tetrahydropyrans
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the alkene moiety to a radical cation, which is intercepted by the tethered alcohol; the resulting C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a cation that is trapped by the external nucleophile (imine N or carboxylate O)

key_intermediates.name: alkene-derived radical cation (Ia for n=1, Ib for n=2)
tetrahydrofuranyl C-centered radical II (n=1, 5-endo-trig from Ia)
tetrahydropyranyl/THF C-centered radical V (n=2, more stable than III)
oxocarbenium/benzylic cation from anodic oxidation of II/V
ester product Pb (with RCOOH nucleophile, 5-exo-trig pathway for n=2)

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the alkene to a radical cation; the pendant hydroxyl cyclizes (5-endo-trig for n=1, 5-exo-trig for n=2) generating a C-centered radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; the external nucleophile (Ar2CNH or RCOOH) traps this cation, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the alkene to the radical cation (initial SET)

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of alkene S2, diphenylmethanimine, and acetic acid
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks of diphenylmethanimine (~2.60 V) and acetic acid (~2.80 V) are higher than that of the alkene C=C (~1.6 V using methoxy analogue S2-I/S2-II), indicating the alkene is oxidized first; cyclization proceeds via the alkene radical cation rather than free iminyl or acyloxy radicals

probe_reagents.name: O2 / air atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Presence of O2 or air significantly inhibited the reaction, consistent with a radical-cation/radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Current and time variation
probe_reagents.observation: Both decrease and increase in current/time lowered yield, consistent with insufficient turnover or overoxidation

notes: Two cyclization modes are proposed from the alkene radical cation: Path A (5-endo-trig, favored for n=1 via more stable radical II) gives product Pa; Path B (5-exo-trig, favored for n=2 via more stable radical V) gives Pb. For n=1, 5-exo radical IV is unstable; for n=2, 5-endo radical III is less stable than V. No TEMPO/BHT radical-trap or KIE experiments are reported on these pages; mechanism is supported primarily by CV and product selectivity.
